headline: Big Bank Earnings Roll In After Goldman Sets the Tone

headline: Wells Fargo Earnings Beat Wall Street Forecasts. Stock Slips.
summary: Wells Fargo reported first-quarter earnings per share of $1.60, beating Wall Street estimates of $1.58. Revenue came in at $21.4 billion, slightly below expectations of $21.8 billion, according to estimates compiled by FactSet.

content.title: Wells Fargo Earnings Beat Wall Street Forecasts. Stock Slips.

headline: Wells Fargo Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 14, 2026--Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) has released its first quarter 2026 financial results. The financial results are available online at https://www.wellsfargo.com/about/investor-relations/quarterly-earnings/ and on a Form 8-K filed by the company with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on April 14, 2026, and available on the SEC’s website at https://www.sec.gov/.

content.title: Wells Fargo Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
content.summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 14, 2026--Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) has released its first quarter 2026 financial results. The financial results are available online at https://www.wellsfargo.com/about/investor-relations/quarterly-earnings/ and on a Form 8-K filed by the company with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on April 14, 2026, and available on the SEC’s website at https://www.sec.gov/.

headline: Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) Misses Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations
summary: Financial services giant Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 6% year on year to $21.45 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.60 per share was 1.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Wells Fargo earnings beat estimates despite revenue miss
summary: Investing.com -- Wells Fargo & Co. (NYSE:WFC) reported first-quarter earnings that exceeded analyst expectations, though revenue fell short of estimates, sending shares down 1.7% premarket.

headline: Wells Fargo's Net Interest Income in First Quarter Misses Estimates
summary: Wells Fargo reported first-quarter net interest income of $12.1 billion, slightly below Wall Street forecasts of $12.3 billion, according to estimates compiled by FactSet.  Net interest income is the difference between what a bank pays customers in interest on deposits and what it earns on interest-bearing assets such as loans.  Wells Fargo reiterated its guidance for net interest income for 2026, which it expects to be around $50 billion.

content.title: Wells Fargo's Net Interest Income in First Quarter Misses Estimates
content.summary: Wells Fargo reported first-quarter net interest income of $12.1 billion, slightly below Wall Street forecasts of $12.3 billion, according to estimates compiled by FactSet.  Net interest income is the difference between what a bank pays customers in interest on deposits and what it earns on interest-bearing assets such as loans.  Wells Fargo reiterated its guidance for net interest income for 2026, which it expects to be around $50 billion.

headline: Wells Fargo's First-Quarter Profit Rises 7%
summary: Wells Fargo said its profit increased 7% from a year earlier at the start of 2026.    Net income at the bank was $5.25 billion in the first quarter. That amounted to $1.60 a share, above analyst estimates of $1.

content.title: Wells Fargo's First-Quarter Profit Rises 7%
content.summary: Wells Fargo said its profit increased 7% from a year earlier at the start of 2026.    Net income at the bank was $5.25 billion in the first quarter. That amounted to $1.60 a share, above analyst estimates of $1.

headline: Wells Fargo Raises Its Provision for Credit Losses
summary: Wells Fargo is setting aside more money in case customers fall behind on their loan payments.  The bank reported its provision for credit losses increased 22% year over year to $1.1 billion for the first quarter, up 9% from the fourth quarter.  Wells Fargo reported that customers were generally not falling behind on their credit card bills.

content.title: Wells Fargo Raises Its Provision for Credit Losses
content.summary: Wells Fargo is setting aside more money in case customers fall behind on their loan payments.  The bank reported its provision for credit losses increased 22% year over year to $1.1 billion for the first quarter, up 9% from the fourth quarter.  Wells Fargo reported that customers were generally not falling behind on their credit card bills.

headline: Earnings: Big Banks navigating complex risks 'relatively well'
summary: Earnings season is off to the races for Big Banks, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) all reporting results that beat quarterly estimates on Tuesday. Citizens director of financial technology research Devin Ryan comes on Market Catalysts to gauge the "increasingly complex set of risks" for banks that Jamie Dimon outlined in JPMorgan's earnings statement.

content.title: Earnings: Big Banks navigating complex risks 'relatively well'
content.description:
Earnings season is off to the races for Big Banks, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) all reporting results that beat quarterly estimates on Tuesday.

Citizens director of financial technology research Devin Ryan comes on Market Catalysts to gauge the "increasingly complex set of risks" for banks that Jamie Dimon outlined in JPMorgan's earnings statement.
content.summary: Earnings season is off to the races for Big Banks, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) all reporting results that beat quarterly estimates on Tuesday. Citizens director of financial technology research Devin Ryan comes on Market Catalysts to gauge the "increasingly complex set of risks" for banks that Jamie Dimon outlined in JPMorgan's earnings statement.

headline: Citigroup Reports $22 Billion of Private-Credit Exposure
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Citigroup Inc. said its exposure to private-credit firms was $22 billion in the fourth quarter, offering a look at what has become a closely watched development by investors.The loans are part of the bank’s portfolio of lending to financial firms other than banks, which totaled $118 billion at the end of last year, Citigroup said Tuesday in a presentation. The bank also said its corporate private-credit warehouse financing has “substantial equity cushions” as well as structural pr

headline: Big Banks Offer New Details on Exposure to Private Credit
summary: Banks sought to reassure investors on Tuesday by giving more detail on their lending to private credit funds in their earnings reports, and described the steps they have taken to limit their risk exposure.  Banks, though, have argued that this lending is highly structured and protected by collateral that the banks pick themselves.  Within that category, about 23% of the exposure was to business-development companies, or BDCs.

content.title: Big Banks Offer New Details on Exposure to Private Credit
content.summary: Banks sought to reassure investors on Tuesday by giving more detail on their lending to private credit funds in their earnings reports, and described the steps they have taken to limit their risk exposure.  Banks, though, have argued that this lending is highly structured and protected by collateral that the banks pick themselves.  Within that category, about 23% of the exposure was to business-development companies, or BDCs.

headline: U.S. Economy Remains Resilient in Face of Iran War, Banks Say
summary: American households and businesses are holding up despite a turbulent start to the year that has threatened to roil the U.S. economy.  JPMorgan Chase Citigroup  and  Wells Fargo  said customers continued to spend, borrow and invest in the first quarter, even as a war erupted in the Middle East and worries mounted about risks from artificial intelligence, inflation and private credit.  JPMorgan Chief Executive  Jamie Dimon  warned that while the broader U.S. economy was largely in good shape, a “complex set of risks” threatened to derail its trajectory.

content.title: U.S. Economy Remains Resilient in Face of Iran War, Banks Say
content.summary: American households and businesses are holding up despite a turbulent start to the year that has threatened to roil the U.S. economy.  JPMorgan Chase Citigroup  and  Wells Fargo  said customers continued to spend, borrow and invest in the first quarter, even as a war erupted in the Middle East and worries mounted about risks from artificial intelligence, inflation and private credit.  JPMorgan Chief Executive  Jamie Dimon  warned that while the broader U.S. economy was largely in good shape, a “complex set of risks” threatened to derail its trajectory.

headline: Bank Earnings Show Consumer Resilience, Even as Pressures Mount
summary: When it comes to describing the U.S. economy, bank executives’ mot du jour is “resilient.”  “What we are seeing right now is that this is a resilient economy,” said Mike Santomassimo, Wells Fargo’s chief financial officer, on a call with reporters.  "The U.S. economy remained resilient in the quarter, with consumers still earning and spending and businesses still healthy,” JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said in a statement.

content.title: Bank Earnings Show Consumer Resilience, Even as Pressures Mount
content.summary: When it comes to describing the U.S. economy, bank executives’ mot du jour is “resilient.”  “What we are seeing right now is that this is a resilient economy,” said Mike Santomassimo, Wells Fargo’s chief financial officer, on a call with reporters.  "The U.S. economy remained resilient in the quarter, with consumers still earning and spending and businesses still healthy,” JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said in a statement.

headline: Wells Fargo (WFC) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Revenue growth was driven by a 5% increase in net interest income and an 8% increase in noninterest income.  Consumer Banking and Lending revenue grew 7% and Commercial Banking revenue grew 7% as well.  Within our Corporate and Investment Bank, we saw an 11% increase in banking revenue and a 19% increase in markets revenue.

content.title: Wells Fargo (WFC) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
content.summary: Revenue growth was driven by a 5% increase in net interest income and an 8% increase in noninterest income.  Consumer Banking and Lending revenue grew 7% and Commercial Banking revenue grew 7% as well.  Within our Corporate and Investment Bank, we saw an 11% increase in banking revenue and a 19% increase in markets revenue.

headline: Wall Street Traders Capitalize on Market Turbulence Tied to Iran War
summary: Middle East conflict and surging geopolitical tensions rattled markets in the first quarter.  JPMorgan Chase said Tuesday that its markets revenue jumped by 20% a year ago to $11.6 billion, a record, lifted by strong client activity across asset classes.  Volatile markets that prompt bank clients to reposition their portfolios and make big bets are typically boons for trading businesses.

content.title: Wall Street Traders Capitalize on Market Turbulence Tied to Iran War
content.summary: Middle East conflict and surging geopolitical tensions rattled markets in the first quarter.  JPMorgan Chase said Tuesday that its markets revenue jumped by 20% a year ago to $11.6 billion, a record, lifted by strong client activity across asset classes.  Volatile markets that prompt bank clients to reposition their portfolios and make big bets are typically boons for trading businesses.

headline: Investment Banking Units Shine During Volatile Quarter
summary: Wall Street’s premier investment banking units put in solid performances during the first quarter, according to earnings reports from three of the nation’s largest banks on Tuesday.  JPMorgan Chase said that investment banking revenue rose 38% year over year to $3.1 billion for the quarter.  Citigroup said investment banking revenue increased 19% to $1.3 billion.

headline: Why Wells Fargo (WFC) Stock Is Nosediving
summary: Shares of financial services giant Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) fell 5% in the afternoon session after the company reported underwhelming first-quarter earnings: Its net interest income missed, and its revenue fell short of Wall Street’s estimates.

content.title: Why Wells Fargo (WFC) Stock Is Nosediving
content.summary: Shares of financial services giant Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) fell 5% in the afternoon session after the company reported underwhelming first-quarter earnings: Its net interest income missed, and its revenue fell short of Wall Street’s estimates.

headline: Morgan Stanley and BofA Expected to Post Higher Profits After Big Banks’ Mixed Results
summary: Investors are set to hear from Bank of America and Morgan Stanley on Wednesday after a string of big banks’ first-quarter earnings reports reflected solid U.S. consumer health.  Results from JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo showed that measures of consumer credit, such as net charge-offs and delinquencies, were steady in the first quarter, even as borrowers faced higher energy prices prompted by the U.S.-Iran war.  Shares of Wells Fargo sunk 5.7% after the firm missed Wall Street's revenue and net interest income expectations, while JPMorgan's stock fell 0.8% and Citi's rose 2.6%.

headline: Market Minute 4-14-26- Big Finance Delivers Big Profits
summary: Stocks staged a solid intraday rally yesterday, though they’re subdued in the early going today. Ditto for Treasuries. Crude oil is dipping a bit along with the US dollar, while gold and silver are rising.
